title: Bulbophyllum cernuum
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum cernuum is a species within the genus Bulbophyllum and the family Orchidaceae. The genus Bulbophyllum is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae, which also includes other orchid genera such as Dendrobium and Coelogyne. Bulbophyllum cernuum can be further classified under the order Asparagales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes asparagus, lilies, and grasses. The order Asparagales is part of the class Liliopsida, also known as monocots. The class Liliopsida is a group of flowering plants that also includes palms, sedges, and dracaenas.

intro: Bulbophyllum cernuum, also known as the Inverted Bell Orchid, is a rare and exotic orchid species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
